mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near cycle lows signals accommodative monetary policy that lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days whenever FEDFUNDS remains below its trailing 12-month average.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or returns during low-rate periods are not statistically higher than high-rate periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month spike in UNRATE above a 0.5 percentage point threshold signals an abrupt deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers a short-horizon risk-off repricing in equities, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered UNRATE spike instances over the walk-forward sample, or the effect is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO growth decelerates while PAYEMS continues to expand, the divergence signals late-cycle labor hoarding amid weakening industrial output, foreshadowing a quarter-ahead earnings disappointment that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism requires the joint condition of falling INDPRO momentum and positive PAYEMS growth; neither series alone carries the signal.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth turns negative while PAYEMS 3-month growth remains positive
failure_condition: Conditional Rank IC for the joint INDPRO-PAYEMS divergence signal is not materially different from the unconditional single-series ICs, or forward 60d returns are positive in > 55% of triggered divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) during a late-cycle expansion regime signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand that precedes broader consumption slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; the same decline in an early-recovery regime is a noisy, non-predictive signal.
failure_condition: The negative HOUST-return relationship fails to hold with statistical significance (p > 0.10) when tested only within independently identified late-cycle expansion subsamples, or reverses sign in more than one out of three rolling regime windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation crosses above a 3.5% threshold, markets price in accelerated future rate hikes, compressing equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days following the CPI release.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following CPI-triggered inflation crossings are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the average triggered return is not significantly different from the unconditional mean (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy income and consumption base that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days whenever PAYEMS growth remains positive on a trailing 3-month basis.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing PAYEMS growth and forward 20d SPX return is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-growth periods do not show significantly higher mean returns than negative-growth periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates concurrently with real GDP (GDPC1) growth, the joint confirmation of consumer and aggregate output expansion signals durable demand strength that supports sustained equity appreciation over the next 60 trading days; RSAFS acceleration without corresponding GDPC1 growth is considered a false positive.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth > 1% AND GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 2%
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS-GDPC1 trigger produces forward 60d returns statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional baseline (p > 0.10), or single-series RSAFS-only signals perform equally well, negating the need for interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) occurring specifically during a monetary tightening regime (rising FEDFUNDS) signals that tighter financial conditions are actively curtailing real output, amplifying downside risk and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days; the same INDPRO decline during an easing regime does not carry predictive power.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM decline while FEDFUNDS rising over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO-return relationship fails to hold with statistical significance (p > 0.10) when isolated to tightening-regime subsamples, or the effect size is not larger than in easing-regime subsamples.
